$comment: Holo Terms MyTerms (IEEE 7012-2025) natively encoded for Hologram OS: the user is the first party and proposes machine-readable personal terms; each app is the second party and is granted only what was agreed, enforced at the capability seam (holo-launch.mount). Hand-authored (like apps/liberty) NOT generated from the hub catalog, so tools/gen-app-defs.mjs never touches it. Built by tools/build-app.mjs.
name: Holo Terms
summary: Your machine-readable personal terms (MyTerms / IEEE 7012): you propose, each app agrees, the OS enforces.

description.p: You should set the terms of your own data and have the OS enforce them, not merely display them.
You pick a standing term from a standard roster; each app must agree before the OS grants it any capability, and you can see and revoke every grant.

ul: Apps must agree before they are granted anything
See and revoke what each app holds
Enforced by the OS, not advisory

resources.name: Holo Terms roster (UOR)
resources.description: The five IEEE 7012-2025 agreements (Customer Commons roster) as content-addressed UOR objects: SD-BASE, SD-BASE-DP, PDC-AI, PDC-GOOD, PDC-INTENT. Each carries its plain-language statement + machine-readable policy and re-derives to its did:holo (Law L5).

tools.name: standing_term
tools.description: Return the user's current standing term (the relationship agreement they propose to every app) as a content-addressed roster object.

tools.name: agreement_for
tools.description: Return the signed agreement record for an app (the granted capabilities under the user's terms), or null. Args: appId.
